data.title: Trump insists Big Tech like Microsoft cover data center power bills to prevent burdening Americans, no specifics revealed.
data.description: President Donald Trump revealed on Monday that his administration is collaborating with prominent U.S. tech firms, especially Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT), to address the issue of data center power consumption impact on Americans. In a statement on Truth Social, Trump expressed his concern by stating, "I never want Americans to face increased electricity costs due to data centers." He emphasized that the U.S. leads in AI and identified data centers as crucial for this advancement but stressed that tech companies should cover their own expenses. Trump hinted at forthcoming "significant adjustments" to be disclosed starting this week to ensure that Americans are not financially burdened by the power consumption of these data centers. The full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Karolinska Development AB begins subscription period for rights issue, restricted in certain jurisdictions.
data.description: This press release is restricted from being released, published or distributed in the United States, Australia, Belarus, Canada, Japan, New Zealand, Russia, Switzerland, Singapore, South Korea, South Africa or any other jurisdiction where such actions would be unlawful. It does not serve as an offer or invitation to acquire or subscribe for any securities in Karolinska Development AB (publ) in any jurisdiction. Please refer to the "Important Information" section below for more details.

data.title: INVL Renewable Energy Fund I repaid EUR 17.5 million loan to Cordiant Capital early, 6 months ahead of schedule.
data.description: RENEWABLE ENERGY FUND I managed by INVL Asset Management, a prominent alternative asset management group in the Baltics, disclosed that their subsidiary, REFI Solar, successfully repaid a significant EUR 17.5 million loan to Cordiant Capital, a global investment management firm. This repayment was made almost six months earlier than the initial maturity date, showcasing REFI Solar's commitment to financial obligations and prudent financial management strategies.

data.title: Litgrid's Board names Andrius Šemeškevičius as CEO of the company; part of EPSO-G group with legal entity code 302826889.
data.description: A new energy company known as EPSO-G has been recently established. With a legal entity code of 302826889, EPSO-G is based in Vilnius, Lithuania, at Laisvės pr. 10.

data.title: FPT Software in Vietnam achieves ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System Accreditation, 2nd in SEA, emphasizing fair AI practices.
data.description: In Hanoi, Vietnam, FPT, a leading IT services company, has obtained ISO/IEC 42001:2023, the inaugural worldwide standard for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Management Systems. This accomplishment positions FPT Software as the pioneer in Vietnam and the second in Southeast Asia to receive this acknowledgment. The certification highlights FPT's dedication to ethical AI advancement and implementation, emphasizing fairness, impartiality, privacy, security, safety, quality, and robustness in their processes.

data.title: X-Sense opens German branch office in Langen, Hesse, near Frankfurt Airport & Central Station to enhance fire safety in Europe.
data.description: X-Sense, a renowned company in smart home and fire safety technology, has recently established its German subsidiary, X-Sense DACH GmbH, in Langen, Hesse, located at Monzastraße 2B. Situated close to Frankfurt Airport and Central Station, this new office serves as a strategic hub for the company's operations. It marks an important milestone in the company's global expansion efforts.
